rationale: Given John's young family and real estate ownership, it's essential to establish a clear plan for the distribution of his assets and the care of his minor children in the event of his passing. A Last Will & Testament and Living Trust will help ensure his wishes are respected, while a Guardianship Designation will provide for his children's well-being. Additionally, a Durable Power of Attorney and HIPAA Authorization will allow his spouse to make medical and financial decisions on his behalf if needed.

As a single 34-year-old business owner, Sanwal needs to prioritize planning for the unexpected, particularly in relation to his business. A Business Succession Plan is crucial to ensure continuity and protection of his business interests. Additionally, basic estate planning documents such as a Last Will & Testament, Durable Power of Attorney, HIPAA Authorization, and Financial Power of Attorney will provide a solid foundation for his overall estate plan.

As a divorced parent with minor children and a business, James needs to ensure his children are protected and his assets are managed properly in case something happens to him. A Last Will & Testament and Guardianship Designation are crucial for naming a guardian for his children and distributing his assets. Additionally, a Business Succession Plan, Durable Power of Attorney, and HIPAA Authorization will help manage his business and personal affairs.
